content.claim: A government decree specifies that district authorities may compel parental compliance with expert assessment for children in dispute, with non-compliance escalating to child-welfare service referral, meaning identification is not purely demand-driven but includes a compulsory state-triggered backstop mechanism.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: The decree text was cited in research notes but specific article numbers and implementation frequency data were not retrieved
No data on the prevalence of compulsory-assessment invocations or child-welfare escalations was found
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
